g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.T213927
g.text: In order for (white and effective) lead white to
come about in the best variety - to be used for
ophthalmological properties - as well as in worse
vinegar into a broad-mouthed jar OR pot-bellied
clay jar, .Place a leaden brick on a lattice of
reeds, .Lay the lattice of reeds with the leaden
brick onto the mouth of the pot, .Above, throw
over covers to prevent vinegar from evaporating,
the clean fluid that floats on top (of the vinegar
after the leaden brick has crumbled down in it),
.Pour the (remaining) viscous fluid into a vessel,
.Let the viscous fluid dry under the sun, .Smooth
the dried substance with a hand-mill OR in any
that is sifted away is the better variety of
(white and effective) lead white, that should be
(with a hand-mill OR in any way) and sift what
remains (above the sieve) of the solid substance
{, repeat, and do it again a third and fourth
time}, .The substances that are sifted away at

g.title: tlg0093_tlg004.P151115
g.text: Place a piece of lead over vinegar in closed jars,
.Wait for lead to acquire thickness .Open the
jars, .Scrape away from the lead the substance
that has formed on its surface, repeating this
until you have consumed it completely, .Grind what
you have scraped away, .Filter off what you are
grinding, .The final precipitate is lead white
